text: HTTPS is essential for secure web development, as it encrypts data between the user’s browser and the server. Authentication, such as strong passwords or two-factor systems, ensures only authorized users access sensitive areas. Input validation is critical to prevent harmful data from entering your site; always check and sanitize user inputs to block attacks like SQL injection. Common vulnerabilities include cross-site scripting and insecure direct object references. Developers must stay vigilant by updating software regularly and testing for weaknesses. By focusing on these core practices, you can build safer websites that protect both users and data from evolving threats.
